body_txt: Something that the Evil Overlord List would agree most of all is not to rely on barking orders at your subordinates. Sometimes, they might be too stupid or too incompetent to do anything . Or rather that kind of barking intimidates them so much that they can't do anything over the fear of what will happen. In either case the subordinates do a poor job, and so, believing now that he can't send minions to do a villain's job, the Evil Overlord instead decides to do things himself. The trope can be used when the Big Bad is above his minions in both power and competence. Usually lampshaded if the villain is Surrounded by Idiots ; at some point after one too many failures, the villain will tend to say "If you want something done right, you gotta do it yourself!" and then proceeds to do far better at any given mission than his underlings have ever done, making you wonder why he didn't just do this in the first place. This trope can start when The Pawns Go First . If the villain gets angry enough to kill his henchman along the way, it's an example of You Have Failed Me . It can be a buildup to a Final Boss , especially if the Big Bad is one. Alternately it's a Final Boss Preview . Subtrope of Let's Get Dangerous! . Contrast Non-Action Big Bad and Orcus on His Throne , when the Big Bad remains on his chair and lets all his subordinates do the work for him. Compare It's Personal , which can be a motivation for this trope.
